# sql-security

> SQL injection screening for host code (MoonBit / TS / Rust) plus secretlint setup notes. Flags single-line template-literal or string-concat SQL builders, regardless of value source — the scanner is line-based and does NOT trace data flow, so a clean scan is not proof of safety (multi-line template literals are missed) and every hit needs a manual review or an explicit `// sql-security: ok` opt-out.

## Install

### APM (recommended)

```bash
apm install mizchi/skills/sql/security
```

Or declare it in `apm.yml` for a reproducible setup:

```yaml
dependencies:
  apm:
    - mizchi/skills/sql/security
```

then run `apm install`.

### Any agentskills.io-compatible agent

This is an [Agent Skills](https://agentskills.io) skill — a portable
`SKILL.md` folder that works with Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Gemini CLI,
Cursor, and other [compatible agents](https://agentskills.io/clients).

With the [`skills`](https://github.com/vercel-labs/skills) CLI (installs into
every detected agent's skills directory):

```bash
npx skills add mizchi/skills --skill sql-security
```

Or copy the folder in directly:

```bash
npx degit mizchi/skills/sql/security ~/.claude/skills/sql-security
```

See [SKILL.md](./SKILL.md) for what this skill does and when to use it.
<!-- apm:readme:end -->
